preamble: The shift from the voiceless uvular fricative [χ] to the voiceless glottal fricative [h] is a process of debuccalization (lenition). It involves moving the place of articulation from the back of the mouth to the glottis, frequently observed in dialects where back fricatives are weakening.

examples.note: The voiceless velar/uvular fricative (jota) debuccalizes to [h] in many coastal dialects of Latin America and Southern Spain.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
